article.title: What happens to Bitcoin if the Nasdaq falls further?
article.summary: Bitcoin eyes a rally toward $92,630 as BTC defends key long-term support while the Nasdaq flashes deeper correction risks.

headline: Saylor Eyes BTC Buy Amid $100M Dividend Vote

article.title: Strategy’s Saylor signals BTC buy as preferred dividend pay date vote looms
article.summary: Strategy’s top execs took to social media on Sunday to tout its Bitcoin strategy while shareholders cast final votes on a proposal for twice-monthly preferred stock dividend payments.

headline: IAG Stocks Plunge 5% on Soaring Fuel Costs

article.title: IAG CEO on Asia demand, Consolidation & Fuel Prices
article.summary: International Airlines Group (IAG) CEO Luis Gallego speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) on growing demand in Asia, consolidation & increasing fuel prices. (Source: Bloomberg)

headline: Japan Inc Debt Surges 20% Amid Deal Spree

article.title: Corporate Japan Borrows More as Deals, Outflows Pressure Ratings
article.summary: Japanese businesses are ramping up borrowing to cover cash shortfalls fueled by record merger activity, booming capital investments and mounting investor pressure for shareholder returns, putting their credit ratings at risk.

headline: Crypto Faces $1B Vulnerability Risk Amid AI Audit

headline: Wall Street Targets Hyperliquid's $1B Perps Dominance

headline: 40,000 Bitcoin Wallets Lawsuit Stayed Until July

article.title: NY judge stays lawsuit seeking ownership of nearly 40,000 bitcoin wallets, sets July hearing on proposed amicus brief
article.summary: The brief, filed by attorney Ian R. Cohen, argues New York's lost-and-found statute cannot be used to claim "lost" assets controlled by private keys.

headline: JPM: $1B Reserve Rebuild Needed for Crypto Stability

article.title: JPMorgan says Strategy may need to rebuild dollar reserves to restore confidence
article.summary: JPMorgan said crypto's outlook depends on Strategy's dividend plan and the Clarity Act, for which it now sees less than a 50% chance of passage this year.
